evals.name: install-single-precompact
evals.prompt:
Run `/hooks install PreCompact` against a fresh project (empty .claude/settings.json). The install must (a) copy hooks/PreCompact-handoff-write.sh to .claude/hooks/, and (b) add a PreCompact entry to settings.json pointing at the copied hook. Use the shared installer at scripts/install-hooks.sh from the Catalyst repo at /path/to/catalyst.

Working dir is fresh — no .claude/ exists. After install, save the resulting settings.json content to `final-settings.json` and list .claude/hooks/ contents to `installed-hooks.txt`.
assertions: final-settings.json contains a PreCompact hook entry
The command field points at PreCompact-handoff-write.sh

evals.name: install-all-four
evals.prompt:
Run `/hooks install --all` against a fresh project. Must install all 4 lifecycle hooks (PreCompact, SessionStart, Stop, UserPromptSubmit). Run it TWICE to verify idempotency (second run should not duplicate entries).

Save the resulting settings.json after both runs to `final-settings.json`.

evals.name: uninstall-removes-cleanly
evals.prompt: Starting state: PreCompact hook already installed. Run `/hooks uninstall PreCompact`. The hook script must be deleted from .claude/hooks/ AND the entry must be removed from .claude/settings.json. Save resulting settings.json to `final-settings.json` and list .claude/hooks/ to `installed-hooks.txt`.
assertions: final-settings.json hooks.PreCompact is empty array OR PreCompact key not present

evals.name: new-event-scaffold
evals.prompt:
Run `/hooks new PostToolUse my-custom-hook`. The skill must scaffold a new hook script at `hooks/PostToolUse-my-custom-hook.sh` with: (a) a header comment naming the event, (b) `set -euo pipefail`, (c) stdin JSON read, (d) jq dependency check, (e) appropriate exit codes documented, (f) a TODO marker for the custom logic.

Save the scaffolded content to `scaffold-output.sh` in your working dir.
assertions: scaffold-output.sh references the PostToolUse event in a comment or output
scaffold-output.sh has a TODO marker for the custom logic

evals.name: lint-catches-bad-matcher
evals.prompt:
Run `/hooks lint <path>` on a hook file that has an overly broad matcher (`.*` which matches every tool). The lint command must flag this as a problem.

The target file `bad-hook.json` (representing a settings.json snippet) is provided. Save the lint output to `lint-output.txt`.
assertions: lint-output.txt indicates this is a warning or error (not silent pass)
